observations.recommendation: 3. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
4. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and conduct regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness.

5. We recommended that the Municipal Assessor update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions and appropriate charges should be filed.

8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Budget Officer to maintain the required Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure, as prescribed, to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials to submit to the Audit Team copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.

13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.
15. We recommended that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers conduct periodic monitoring of submission of required reports to COA, Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and other regulatory bodies and see to it that all the required reports are submitted on time.

We recommended that the management submit a complete set of financial statements condensed and by fund cluster including the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as required under Chapter 19 Financial Reporting, Section 5 of GAM Volume I and Section 41 Paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS) and supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days from the receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS must be regularly submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots within ten days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal entry voucher (JEVs) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

We recommended that the management mark all motor vehicles of the Municipality with “For Official Use Only” and to exert an effort that existing motor vehicles which are not yet government plated be registered accordingly under which place the name of the Municipality.

We reiterate this recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list of taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card and make an effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; adjust the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and accurate balance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, direct the Municipal Accountant to timely submit the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements and related supporting documents to the source agencies to avoid non-reconciliation of balances as well as to avoid the risk that they may not be able to be given additional funding from the National Government.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

We have recommended furnishing the Auditor copy of the POs and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection and issuance of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation.

We therefore recommended that the management to include the Audit Team in the acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of the agency and the submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel concerned to submit the list of PPAs of the Municipality, annually at the beginning of the year; and prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the Management should instruct the HRMO Department to submit current plantilla of personnel every year and furnish the Audit Team copies of agency personnel movements (i.e. approved appointment/contract of service of every newly hired, promoted as well as the newly renewed contract) beforehand.

We recommended that Management should require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to the Audit Team for review and verification.

Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the corresponding AR to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and
b) Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of disbursements.

Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 and refrain from appropriating or including in the appropriation of development projects the personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the general fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities for easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the funds pursuant to Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular No. 125 and Section 141 of the GAM for LGUs.

2. We recommended that the required monthly fund utilization reports and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format be immediately posted in the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality.

7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer attach the payroll or list of payees in case of cash advances for payroll and the approved budget in case of cash advances for field/activity COE.
10. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the guidelines pertaining to the submission of and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports.
11. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.

12. Demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant within the tome prescribed by the regulations.
14. We recommended that the Municipal Government maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

16. The LGU should comply with provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 on Information and Publicity on Programs/ Projects/ Activities (PPA) of government agencies, particularly on the posting of Signboards specified in Annex “A” and the general guidelines, and reporting and monitoring.
17. LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance to the requirements under Section 53 of the IRR-A of Republic Act No. 9184.
18. Management must comply with the requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulation. The BAC shall be required to submit quarterly report of activities related to procurement.

19. It is recommended that the municipal government immediately require its Municipal Assessor to make assessment of real properties based on the method prescribed by law. Major improvements, however, like building an addition to an existing home or building a home on a vacant piece of land can trigger reassessment and there-fore an increase in property tax levied.

Create an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properlydisposed and dropped from the books. - Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and conduct regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness.

The Municipal Mayor should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions and appropriate charges should be filed.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.
The Municipal Government should maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

Require the Budget Officer to maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure, as prescribed, to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

Submit to the Audit Team copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.

Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.
We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the timely submission of reports to facilitate the recording of the same in the books of accounts and submission to the Auditor’s Office within ten (10) days after receipt thereof.
LGU should procure commonly used goods and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance to the requirements under Section 53(e) of the IRR-A of Republic Act No. 9184.

Management must comply with the requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules & Regulations. The BAC shall be required to submit quarterly report of supplies and activities related to procurement.
It is recommended that the municipal government immediately require its Municipal Assessor to make assessment of real properties based on the method prescribed by law.

general fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities for easy
monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the funds pursuant to Section 3.7 of Local
Budget Circular No. 125 and Section 141 of the GAM for LGUs.

implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format be immediately posted in at
least three (3) conspicuous publicplaces in the locality.

We recommended that the Municipal Government maintain special accounts in the

Submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD: 1) the annual LDRRMFIP for 2019 presenting the lump-sum 30% allocation for Quick Response Fund and allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded 2) a monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 15th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

Management asserted that procuring supplies at Zamboanga Depot is costlier compared to procuring locally procuring supplies as this would entail additional delivery costs.
Prices of supplies are almost at par with those in Zamboanga City.

inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be
immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of

Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and to
conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.

   properly disposed and dropped from the books.


   We also recommend that  the  LCE  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  and
   Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE  Ledger  Cards  and  Property  Cards,
   respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to
   ensure correctness.

The  Municipal  Mayor  should  create  a  committee  to   undertake   the
   reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation  with
   the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they  should  come
   up with a commitment on how  to  remit  the  long  outstanding  premiums.
   However, those officials responsible  for  not  remitting  the  mandatory
   contribution should be  answerable  for  their  actions  and  appropriate
   charges should be filed.

Management should comply with the maximum limit of government  share  for
   Pag-IBIG Contribution based on the two (2%) of  P  5,000  or  P  100  per
   employee per month pursuant to the provisions of RA 9679  and  RA  10964,
   and cause the refund from the employees concerned of the over  remittance
   of Pag-BIG employer’s share premiums.

The Budget Officer  should  maintain  the  Registries  of  Appropriation,
   Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure as  prescribed  to
   ensure the preparation of the  Statement  of  Comparison  of  Budget  and
   Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal
   control on obligation of expenses.

The LGU should comply with the provisions of COA  Circular  No.  2013-004
   dated   January   30,   2013   on   Information    and    Publicity    on
   Programs/Projects/Activities (PPA) of government  agencies,  particularly
   on the posting of Signboards specified  in  Annex  “A”  and  the  general
   guidelines, and reporting and monitoring.

The Municipal Government should maintain special accounts in the  General
   Fund for the 20% Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master  List  of  Real  Property
   Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an  accurate  basis  in  the
   preparation of a certified list showing the names of  taxpayers  and  the
   amount due and collectible for the year.  The certified list shall be the
   basis of  the  Municipal  Accountant  in  setting  up  the  RPT  and  SET
   Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the timely submission of reports to facilitate the recording of the same in the books of accounts and submission to the Audi-tor’s Office within ten (10) days after receipt thereof.

The LGU should exert effort to secure the documents necessary to recon-struct the PPE accounts so that depreciation for the current and previous years maybe computed to arrive at the correct valuation of the property, plant and equipment. In case the necessary data could no longer be secured after exhausting all possible means, we recommend that appraisal of the PPEs be considered in order to establish the correct book values.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly bank reconci-liation statement and submit same to the Office of the Auditor promptly and regularly.

Require the LGU to established Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) in the municipality and a Barangay Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (BDRRMO) in every barangay which shall be responsible for setting the direction, development, implementation and coor-dination of disaster risk management programs within their territorial jurisdiction.

It is recommended  
that the municipal 
government         
imme-diately

Recommendation:


   We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic  physical
   inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon
   be immediately prepared and submitted  to  the  Auditor  not  later  than
   January 31 of each year .  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be
   properly disposed and dropped from the books.


   We also recommend that  the  LCE  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  and
   Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE  Ledger  Cards  and  Property  Cards,
   respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to
   ensure correctness.

Recommendation:


   We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-
   390 dated November 17, 1992.

Recommendation:


   The Municipal Assessor should update the Master  List  of Real  Property
   Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an  accurate  basis  in  the
   preparation of a certified list showing the names of  taxpayers  and  the
   amount due and collectible for the year.  The certified list shall be the
   basis of  the  Municipal  Accountant  in  setting  up  the  RPT  and  SET
   Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:


   The Municipal Government should maintain special accounts in the  General
   Fund for the 20% Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

Recommendation:


   The  Municipal  Mayor  should  create  a  committee  to   undertake   the
   reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation  with
   the GSIS on the interest
   due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with  a  commitment  on
   how to remit the long  outstanding  premiums.  However,  those  officials
   responsible for  not  remitting  the  mandatory  contribution  should  be
   answerable for their actions and appropriate charges should be filed.

Recommendation:


   The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the  Municipal  Treasurer
   for the timely submission of reports to facilitate the recording  of  the

   The LGU should submit  its  GAD  Accomplishment  Report/s  to  facilitate

   and substantive audit of GAD related transactions.
